description:
Trigger a Precisely Connect ETL job-run via the ``/runtask`` endpoint.

Example:
    ```yaml
    type: dagster_community_components.PreciselyConnectJobTriggerComponent
    attributes:
      asset_name: precisely_load_customers_run
      job_id: "abc-123-xyz"
      host_env_var: PRECISELY_HOST
      api_token_env_var: PRECISELY_API_TOKEN
      parameters:
        INPUT_FILE: /data/customers.csv
        BATCH_DATE: "2026-05-27"
    ```

The returned jobRunId is captured as asset metadata so a paired
``precisely_job_sensor`` can read it from the latest materialization
and watch for terminal SUCCESS — closing the submit + watch loop.

asset_key.description: Dagster asset key for the Precisely job (e.g. 'precisely/etl/load_customers'). Use '/' separators for nested keys. Same value an ``external_precisely_job`` would use — but do NOT use both components on the same key; pick the Case (A or B in the module docstring) that matches your schedule ownership.
asset_key.title: Asset Key

job_id.description: Precisely Connect ETL stable job id (the job to trigger).
job_id.title: Job Id

host_env_var.description: Env var with Precisely Connect ETL host URL.
host_env_var.title: Host Env Var

api_token_env_var.description: Env var with Precisely API bearer token.
api_token_env_var.title: Api Token Env Var

parameters.description: Optional Connect ETL task parameters passed as JSON body to /runtask. Shape is job-specific (defined in DMExpress / Connect ETL UI); examples include INPUT_FILE, BATCH_DATE, etc.
parameters.title: Parameters

timeout_seconds.description: HTTP timeout for the submit POST.
timeout_seconds.title: Timeout Seconds

title: PreciselyConnectJobTriggerComponent
